etymology.description: semantic 忄(心) (xīn, "heart") + phonetic 圣(聖) (shèng, "saint") -> weird; strange
etymology.interpretation: The character combines 忄(心) (xīn, "heart"), suggesting a feeling or emotion, with 圣(聖) (shèng, "saint"), which phonetically suggests the sound of the word. The combination implies a sense of an emotional response to something unusual or unexpected, leading to meanings like "weird" or "strange." The extension to "blame" and "monster" comes from attributing strangeness or malice when things are off or different.

examples.chinese: 这个故事真是太怪了(這個故事真是太怪了)！
examples.pinyin: Zhège gùshi zhēnshi tài guài le!
examples.english: This story is really too weird!

examples.chinese: 他总是怪天气不好(他總是怪天氣不好)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zǒng shì guài tiānqì bù hǎo.
examples.english: He always blames the weather for being bad.

examples.chinese: 在森林里遇到怪兽(在森林里遇到怪獸)，真是令人毛骨悚然(真是令人毛骨悚然)。
examples.pinyin: Zài sēnlín lǐ yùdào guàishòu, zhēnshi lìngrén máogǔsǒngrán.
examples.english: Encountering a monster in the forest is truly spine-chilling.
